story_clusters.summary: Amit Shah, India’s Home Minister, has predicted the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) will win 110 of 152 seats in the first phase of polling in West Bengal, aiming to form the state government. Mamata Banerjee, West Bengal’s Chief Minister, plans to file a legal case against Shah over his remarks. The sources agree Shah is targeting West Bengal, a state where he previously served as a leader.

story_clusters.summary: The Supreme Court directed West Bengal poll duty officers excluded from the voter list to approach Appellate Tribunals, while the Election Commission reported a record 93.19% turnout in the first phase of the state assembly elections. The Supreme Court also praised the elections for an "encouraging turnout and no violence," despite the poll panel imposing unprecedented restrictions such as bans on bikes, liquor, and outsiders.

articles.title: Supreme Court Directs States To Prepare Action Plans To Implement Minimum Standards For Intensive Care Units
articles.summary: The Supreme Court directed all Indian states and Union Territories to prepare action plans for implementing minimum standards for Intensive Care Units (ICUs) within three weeks. The Court ordered health department officials to convene meetings with experts to develop a national blueprint based on a common framework. This directive aims to standardize critical care infrastructure across the country.

articles.title: Calcutta HC Quashed Property Tax Demand Due to Invalid Retrospective Validation Clause
articles.summary: The Calcutta High Court set aside a property tax demand because a retrospective validation clause in the law was deemed invalid. The ruling emphasized that amended tax provisions cannot be used to reopen cases already settled by final court judgments. This decision underscores the importance of judicial finality and constitutional safeguards in retrospective fiscal laws.
articles.raw_summary: The ruling held that amended provisions cannot be used to reopen cases already settled by final judgments. It emphasizes that retrospective fiscal laws must respect judicial finality and constitutional safeguards. The post Calcutta HC Quashed Property Tax Demand Due to Invalid Retrospective Validation Clause appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Rising Global Uncertainty: Why Repo Rate Was Kept Unchanged at 5.25%
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) decided to keep the repo rate unchanged at 5.25% amid rising global uncertainty caused by geopolitical tensions and supply shocks. The Committee cited the need for a cautious, data-driven approach to policy-making. The decision reflects the MPC's response to current economic challenges and aims to balance growth and inflation concerns.
articles.raw_summary: The case examines the MPC’s response to geopolitical tensions and supply shocks. The Committee held rates steady, citing uncertainty and the need for a cautious, data-driven approach. The post Rising Global Uncertainty: Why Repo Rate Was Kept Unchanged at 5.25% appeared first on TaxGuru .

The ruling impacts businesses and tax authorities interpreting GST rules on leasehold transactions.

Singh also mentioned that open market operations will continue to manage system liquidity, while emphasizing India's strong domestic growth fundamentals despite possible supply shocks.

articles.title: Why GST Crackdown Is Targeting Easy Taxpayers Instead of Fraudsters?
articles.summary: The article examines how aggressive Goods and Services Tax (GST) enforcement actions are disproportionately affecting legitimate businesses rather than targeting actual tax fraudsters. articles.title: GST Demand Cannot Exceed Show Cause Notice: Telangana HC
articles.summary: The Telangana High Court ruled that tax authorities cannot demand an amount exceeding what is specified in a show cause notice under GST. The court held that such excess demand violates Section 75(7) of the GST law and directed authorities to rectify the demand through proper procedure. The ruling clarifies the legal limits of tax demands and ensures procedural fairness in GST administration.
articles.raw_summary: The issue was whether tax demand can exceed the amount and scope mentioned in the show cause notice. The Court held that such excess demand violates Section 75(7) and directed rectification through proper procedure. articles.title: Polycotton Recycling Faces a Scale Test That Chemistry Alone Cannot Solve
articles.summary: The article examines the challenges of recycling polycotton blended textiles, framing it as an industrial systems problem rather than just a scientific one. Peter Mangnus of Avantium argues that solving this requires aligning feedstock, offtake, finance, and policy support. The debate underscores the need for regulatory and infrastructure interventions to address textile waste at scale. The discussion is relevant to India's textile sector and waste management policies.
articles.raw_summary: Blended textile waste is no longer being discussed as a marginal recycling problem but as an industrial systems challenge shaped by regulation, cost and infrastructure. In that debate, Peter Mangnus , Business Director Dawn Technology at Avantium , argues that progress will depend less on scientific novelty alone than on aligning feedstock, offtake, finance and policy support.

articles.title: Service Tax Not Applicable on Forex Profit: Refund Denial on Limitation Rejected by Madras HC

The ruling impacts tax administration and clarifies the legal position on service tax for forex profits.

articles.title: 'Service Inam' Land Attached To Mosque Is Wakf Property, Can't Be Alienated : Supreme Court

The court emphasized that these lands are not alienable, reinforcing protections for Wakf properties under Indian law.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court on Friday (April 24) has observed that lands attached to Mosques known as 'service imams' form part of the Wakf Property, and therefore cannot be alienated. “It is undisputed and settled that lands granted as service inam for religious or charitable purposes partake the character of endowed property and are impressed with a public or religious trust, thereby restricting their alienability.” , observed a bench of Justice MM Sundresh and Justice Augustine George Masih. articles.title: West Bengal polls: Clashes, attacks on candidates mar phase 1 voting across 152 seats
articles.summary: During the first phase of West Bengal's elections across 152 seats, clashes and attacks on candidates Subhendu Sarkar and Agnimitra Paul occurred, alongside allegations of EVM tampering and voter intimidation. Voter turnout exceeded 62%, but the incidents prompted the Election Commission to seek reports from authorities. The violence and administrative disputes raise concerns about electoral fairness and safety, which may lead to further scrutiny or policy measures by election authorities.
articles.raw_summary: West Bengal Phase 1 polls across 152 seats saw clashes, attacks on candidates Subhendu Sarkar and Agnimitra Paul, EVM row and voter intimidation even as turnout crossed 62%, prompting the Election Commission to seek reports.

articles.title: Tamil Nadu Polls 2026: What all MK Stalin, Edappadi K. Palaniswami and Vijay own
articles.summary: Tamil Nadu will hold state elections on April 23, 2026, featuring a three-way contest between the ruling DMK led by Chief Minister MK Stalin, the opposition AIADMK led by Edappadi K. Palaniswami, and actor-turned-politician Vijay, whose debut and substantial assets contrast with the modest financial declarations of the other two leaders. The election outcome could shape the state's policy direction over the next five years. Vijay's entry into politics adds a new dynamic to the traditional rivalr
articles.raw_summary: Tamil Nadu votes on April 23 in a three way fight between DMK AIADMK and Vijay, whose debut and large assets contrast with modest declarations by Stalin and Palaniswami

articles.title: West Bengal polls: 23% candidates have criminal cases, 22% crorepatis in second phase, says ADR report
articles.summary: A report by the Association for Democratic Reforms (ADR) ahead of the second phase of West Bengal's polls reveals that 23% of candidates have criminal cases and 22% are crorepatis. It also notes that 44% of seats have multiple candidates with criminal records. The findings raise concerns about electoral integrity and governance, potentially prompting calls for reforms in candidate transparency or election laws.
articles.raw_summary: ADR report says that West Bengal polls phase II to see 23% candidates facing criminal cases, 22% crorepatis and 44% seats are red alert with multiple tainted candidates.

articles.title: Tamil Nadu polls: How sustainable are the freebies parties are raining down
articles.summary: When the 5.67 crore voters of Tamil Nadu head to the polling stations on April 23, they will have a wide array of promises and giveaways to choose from among the parties in the fray. With elections underway, the three main contenders—the DMK, AIADMK and TVK, among others—have rolled out a host of offerings, alongside pledges of good governance. But what does the voter really want? Political observer and writer K. V. Prasad finds out.

articles.summary: A fly-ash dam operated by a plant in Korba, Chhattisgarh, burst in April 2026, allegedly due to negligence. Local residents claim the dam had suffered breaches three times before and had been flagged as unsafe, but management ignored warnings. The incident raises concerns about industrial safety regulations and enforcement. The source, SANDRP, has highlighted the recurring risks associated with such dams.
articles.raw_summary: Terming the incident a result of negligence, local residents have raised questions about the safety measures of fly ash dam by the plant administration. According to them, warnings regarding the precarious condition of the dam had been issued several times but the management ignored them.

The findings could inform India's strategies to combat AMR, a growing public health challenge in the country.

articles.summary: The U.S. announced it is clearing Iranian mines in the Strait of Hormuz to reopen the critical oil transit route. The Trump administration is taking this action amid rising energy prices and broader economic concerns. The strait is a vital chokepoint for global oil shipments, and disruptions could affect India's energy imports and economic stability.

articles.title: ‘Court not theatre of perception’—Justice Sharma’s order refusing Kejriwal’s recusal plea in excise case
articles.summary: Justice Sharma of the Delhi High Court refused Arvind Kejriwal's plea to recuse from the excise policy case, stating that unsubstantiated claims of bias cannot be allowed to undermine judicial independence. The judge emphasized that the judiciary cannot be put on trial based on perceptions alone, setting a precedent for institutional integrity. The order underscores the court's commitment to maintaining public trust in the justice system.
articles.raw_summary: The judge said that apprehension of bias, unsupported by tangible evidence, cannot be allowed to put judiciary itself on trial, or set a precedent that undermines institutional integrity.

The report highlights potential security risks associated with AI systems.

India, a major oil importer, may face economic and policy implications due to these developments.

articles.summary: Three people were killed and a few villagers injured in a fresh gunfight between Naga and Kuki groups in Ukhrul, Manipur, according to police. The conflict highlights ongoing ethnic tensions in the region, which have previously led to demands for stronger government action to protect vulnerable tribal communities. No official numbers were provided for the injured.
articles.raw_summary: The officer said a few villagers were injured in the gunfight but could not confirm the numbers.

articles.title: Delhi's groundwater levels improve, records show higher recharge than use in 2025
articles.summary: Delhi recharged 0.38 bcm groundwater in 2024-25 and extracted 0.32 bcm. The extraction rate improved to 92.1% from over 100% last year.

articles.title: Blackout mock drill conducted across Punjab to assess emergency preparedness
articles.summary: The Punjab government conducted a 15-minute blackout mock drill across the state to test emergency preparedness. Residents complied by avoiding generator use during the exercise. The drill aims to evaluate the state's ability to handle power disruptions and improve response mechanisms. Such drills often inform future policy decisions on energy infrastructure and disaster management.
articles.raw_summary: Local residents fully cooperated with the government's call and refrained from using generators in their homes for the 15-minute duration.

articles.title: Nashik ‘godman’ sexually exploits woman, clicks ‘objectionable’ photos, arrested
articles.summary: A self-proclaimed 'godman' in Nashik was arrested for allegedly sexually assaulting a woman in 2024, taking objectionable photographs of her during the incident. The accused lured the survivor to a lodge before committing the crime. This incident highlights ongoing concerns about gender-based violence and the need for stronger legal protections for women in India.
articles.raw_summary: In 2024, the accused took the survivor to a lodge, where he sexually assaulted her and took her objectionable photographs, police said.

articles.title: Six Pak nationals sentenced to 20 years' imprisonment for drug smuggling
articles.summary: Six Pakistani nationals were sentenced to 20 years in prison by an Indian court for drug smuggling. They were arrested in December 2021 from a Pakistani fishing boat off Gujarat’s coast. The case highlights cross-border drug trafficking challenges and India’s strict anti-narcotics enforcement.

articles.title: Large wildlife habitat worldwide likely to be hit by extreme weather by 2050: Study
articles.summary: A new study published in Nature Ecology and Evolution warns that large wildlife habitats worldwide, including in India, are likely to face increased extreme weather events like heatwaves, fires, and floods by 2050. The research highlights the growing threat to ecosystems and biodiversity due to climate change. While the study is global, its findings could influence India's environmental policies and conservation strategies.

articles.title: Uncertainty shrouds Pakistan over US-Iran talks: What's on agenda amid Islamabad lockdown?
articles.summary: The US and Iran are in a deadlock over oil exports, with Iran blocking the Strait of Hormuz and the US imposing sanctions. The outcome could impact energy prices and diplomatic relations in South Asia.

articles.title: Mint Explainer | Is India's wheat export push a good idea?
